source: Fast Matrix Factorization for Online Recommendation with Implicit Feedback

concepts.finding: Fast incremental MF for implicit feedback. ALS with confidence weighting (observed=high confidence, unobserved=low confidence). Efficient incremental update: when new interaction arrives, closed-form update for affected user and item factors. O(k^2) per update instead of full O(n*k^2). Supports real-time recommendation updates.
concepts.implication: Fast incremental keyword-embedding updates for new keyword-intent observations. When a new keyword-intent pair is observed (e.g., from editorial labeling), keyword and intent factors updated incrementally in O(k^2). Enables real-time keyword model updates.
concepts.open_question: How many concurrent keyword embedding updates can a production system handle per second? Is incremental update latency acceptable for real-time applications?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Incremental keyword embedding updates process new keyword-intent observations in <10ms, enabling real-time keyword model updates as new labeled data arrives.
